issues: Dave Tomlinson (a known Sockeyes player) scored a goal for Boston University, which is the Sockeyes-relevant content — the draft mentions him but buries him incorrectly. The draft states 'Dave Tomlinson and Sullivan also scored' which is inaccurate; Sullivan scored in the first period and had an assist, while the third-period scorers were Tomlinson and Kelfer (not Sullivan again).
The drafted headline 'Mike Kelfer Scores Twice as BU Shuts Out Lowell' does not appear in the OCR — there is no clear standalone headline visible in the OCR text.
The short description omits Dave Tomlinson entirely despite him being the archivally significant Sockeyes-connected player on the page.
The long description incorrectly says 'Dave Tomlinson and Sullivan also scored' — Sullivan did not score a second time; the third-period goals were by Tomlinson and Kelfer.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Former Richmond Sockeyes player Dave Tomlinson scored a third-period goal for Boston University in a 5-0 Hockey East shutout of the University of Lowell at Tully Forum.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: Boston University blanked the University of Lowell 5-0 in the Hockey East opener for both schools at Billerica's Tully Forum on October 31, 1987. Mike Kelfer scored twice and Mike Sullivan had a goal and an assist, while former Richmond Sockeyes player Dave Tomlinson added a third-period goal. Goalie Peter Fish recorded his first career shutout with 29 saves against 29 shots.
review.notes: Dave Tomlinson (North Vancouver, BC) is the Sockeyes-relevant connection here — he scored a goal for BU. The draft mentioned him but misattributed the second scorer in the long description (said Sullivan scored twice; it was Tomlinson and Kelfer in the third period). No clear standalone headline is visible in the OCR. Short description updated to foreground Tomlinson's Sockeyes connection.
